questions.statement: Asymmetric information problem arise-

In vertically integrated firm, but not horizontally<br />
integrated firm. /

questions.statement: Giffin&#39;s Paradox refers to a good whose -
options: quantity demanded varies directly with price. /
quantity demanded varies indirectly with price. /
quantity demanded varies directly with income. /
quantity demanded varies indirectly with income. /

questions.statement: Gross Fixed Capital Formation is the other part of investment along with the part as -
What is the percentage of Net Demand and Time Liabilities (NDTL) that can be lent under MSF facility?
In a fractional Reserve Banking system, an increase in reserve requirements -
options: increases both the money multiplier and the money supply. /
decreases both the money multiplier and the money supply. /
decreases&nbsp;the money&nbsp;multiplier but increases the money supply. /

questions.statement: The gap between public income and expenditure can be bridged by
Excess of public expenditure over public revenue leads to :
questions.explanation: Deficit financing occurs when government spend more than revenue that it receiving, so government has to borrow more from the market to make up deficit .In short, Deficit financing is government policy of financing large public expenditures by borrowing money rather than by raising taxes.

questions.statement: Market Economics rely on which of the following to allocate scarce&nbsp;resources ?
Which of the following is <u>NOT</u> a determinant of the long run level&nbsp;of real GDP ?
The Wealth effect, Interest rate effect and Exchange rate effect are all explanations for -
options: The slope of long run aggregate supply. /
The slope of the aggregate demand curve. /
Everything that makes the&nbsp;aggregate&nbsp;demand curve shift. /

questions.statement: ​If each value in a given set of data is divided by 4, how will it affect Karl Pearson&#39;s coefficient of Skewness?
options: It will reduce the value of co-efficient to one fourth /
It will increase the value of co-efficient to four times /
It will not affect the co-efficient of Skewness value /
Nothing can be said about the change /

questions.statement: Which of the following adjust to bring aggregate supply and demand into balance ?
options: The real rate of interest and&nbsp;money supply. /
The saving rate and Net exports. /

questions.statement: The deviation of unemployment&nbsp;from its natural rate is called
​The theory which explains the effect of devaluation on balance of trade is known as:
Which of the following is correct ?
options: Unemployment rate =&nbsp;<span class="math-tex">\(\frac{\text { Number of unemployed }}{\text { No. of employed }+\ \rm No.\ of \ unemployed} \times 100\)</span> /
Unemployment rate =&nbsp;<span class="math-tex">\(\frac{\text { No. of unemployed }}{\text { Adult population }} \times 100\)</span> /

questions.statement: When price level is fixed in Keynsian model -
options: An increase in money supply increases level of income and reduce rate of interest. /
A fall in money supply increase level of income and reduce the rate of interest. /
An increase in money supply reduces the level of income and rate of interest. /
An increase in money supply increase the level of income and rate of interest. /
